meta.purpose: Validate Migration/Liberation thesis (Option C lane) by probing the export-tool space directly rather than running standard incumbent-anchored find_competitors.

demand_quotes: I'm looking for a solution to sync my web highlights to my Obsidian Vault so I can quickly search my web history (aka my web highlights) while writing notes
EDIT: If only paid solutions exist, I'm looking for solutions without a monthly paid subscription. One time fee is fine though.
I am trying to highlight and annotate web pages. Then sync these highlights to obsidian for further organization. What extension/plugin should I use for it?

queries.notes: All results are either Capacities own first-party docs (5/7) or Microsoft Fabric (2/7 — wrong domain entirely). Zero third-party migration utilities surfaced for this niche.
No tools surfaced that specifically migrate browser highlights to vault destinations. PDF highlight extractors exist but operate on a different source surface.

aggregate.verdict_reasoning: Real direct competitor: Glasp (1). Adjacent competitor: Highlight Extract (PDF/Word, different surface). Both interpretations fall within the directive's <=2 threshold.

demand_signals.signal_pattern: Users actively asking for solutions that don't surface in the search; demand exists, supply is thin.

thesis: Web Highlights users articulate migration intent in public forums
Web Highlights' own docs describe Markdown export — but it's a manual copy-paste, not a programmatic migrator
Glasp is the only third-party tool that programmatically exports web highlights to Obsidian/Notion/Capacities
Capacities migration tooling space has zero third-party tools (all first-party)
Browser-highlight migration as a category has no recognizable product cluster
